Decision: Modify train.py to force CPU, train for 20 epochs to get a solid baseline, and submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: DLinear trained for 20 epochs on CPU, best val loss 1.215777. Training took ~15 min. No eval submitted yet. Analysis: Need to submit eval to get baseline MSE score before making improvements. Key improvements to try: (1) RevIN (Reversible Instance Normalization) is known to greatly help non-stationary TS, (2) individual=True for per-channel linear layers, (3) potentially use Chronos-Bolt-Small for zero-shot forecasting. Decision: Submit eval on current model immediately to get baseline score.

trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline DLinear (individual=False, no RevIN) achieved MSE=0.5038. Training on CPU takes ~15 min for 20 epochs. Analysis: Key improvements to try: (1) individual=True allows per-channel linear layers (7x more params but still <1M), known to significantly help multivariate forecasting; (2) RevIN removes instance-level distribution shifts, very effective for non-stationary time series. Decision: Implement both improvements, train for 25 epochs, submit eval immediately.

trajectory.error_summary: [TIMEOUT] Command exceeded 1506s limit. Consider reducing epochs or using a smaller model.
trajectory.description: Observation: DLinear with RevIN+individual achieved MSE=0.4815. Training took ~16min for 25 epochs on CPU. Model was overfitting (train 0.59, val 1.56). Analysis: DLinear's single linear layer per channel (96→720) has limited capacity and overfits. A PatchTST architecture with patch-based tokenization + transformer attention can capture more complex temporal patterns with fewer parameters, potentially generalizing better. Key improvements: (1) Patching reduces sequence length (96→11 patches), (2) Transformer attention captures long-range dependencies, (3) Shared parameters across channels reduces overfitting, (4) Dropout for regularization. Decision: Implement PatchTST (d_model=128, n_heads=4, n_layers=2, patch_size=16, stride=8, dropout=0.15) with RevIN, train for 25 epochs on CPU, and submit eval.

The PatchTST checkpoint has overwritten the DLinear checkpoint at best.pt. Remaining time budget is 0.0 hours - extremely limited time left. Analysis: The current best.pt contains a PatchTST model (epoch 4) that's undertrained but functional. I should submit eval immediately with this checkpoint to get whatever score it produces, before the experiment ends. Decision: Submit eval with current checkpoint immediately.
